reasoning: The Chicago White Sox had a historically awful 41-121 record in 2024, the worst in modern MLB history. They traded their best player, Garrett Crochet, in the offseason. Oddsmakers have set their 2025 win total around 54.5, by far the lowest in the league, and their odds of winning the AL Pennant are +25000, roughly an implied probability of 0.4%. With virtually no realistic path to the playoffs, let alone a championship, the probability is extremely close to zero. We constrain it to the minimum allowable probability bounds of this forecasting system.
